notes: Articles self-sent to Gmail as research clips, 2024-08 to 2026-05. Starred threads with label Label_2207168012066181003 appear to be the primary research label. URLs are as captured — some are share.google redirects that should be resolved to canonical URLs. Locations are inferred from article subjects where not explicit.

articles.title: AI facial recognition oversight lagging far behind technology, watchdogs warn
articles.notes: URL needs resolving. Oversight/regulation framing — relevant to legal/accountability layer.

articles.title: Man falsely arrested after being misidentified by facial recognition and held in custody for 24 hours in Harlesden
articles.notes: Subject line misspelt 'Harlsede' — confirmed Harlesden from article URL context. New false-positive incident, 24hrs custody. Potentially a new deployment record for data/deployments/.

articles.title: Challenge over Met Police's use of live facial recognition lost
articles.notes: Liberty v Metropolitan Police (21 Apr 2026) — already in enforcement-actions.json as legal-002. URL needs resolving to canonical article.

articles.title: Facial recognition to be 'rolled out' across UK after human rights challenge fails
articles.notes: National expansion announcement following Liberty v Met dismissal. URL needs resolving.

articles.title: Essex Police tweaks 'biased' facial recognition software
articles.notes: Essex Police, not Met — broader UK context for bias/accuracy discourse. Home Office admitted issues with Black and Asian subjects (see news-014).

articles.title: Met police to pilot facial recognition identity checks, mayor confirms
articles.notes: New use case — LFR for identity checks, not just watchlist matching. Different legal and operational framework. Mayor's confirmation gives political accountability hook.

articles.title: Police start live facial recognition trial at London stations
articles.notes: Subject: 'FRS at London bridge'. BBC source. New deployment location — transport hub. Potentially a new deployment record. URL needs resolving to canonical BBC article.

articles.title: 'Orwellian': Sainsbury's staff using facial recognition tech eject innocent shopper
articles.notes: London man ejected from Sainsbury's after Facewatch false positive. Specific store not confirmed from subject line — article may specify. Directly relevant to private-operators.json Facewatch records.

articles.title: How 2 independent grocers are using facial recognition to combat theft
articles.notes: US article (Grocery Outlet, Fiesta Foods) — comparative context for retail FRS deployment patterns.

articles.title: Home Office admits facial recognition tech issue with Black and Asian subjects
articles.notes: Home Office admission of ethnic bias in FR systems. Directly contradicts Met's NPL-testing 'no ethnic bias' claim. High evidential value.

articles.title: No arrests from false facial recognition alerts, Met Police says
articles.notes: Met statement on false alert handling. Claims no one arrested from false positive. Context: multiple documented false positive incidents (Thompson, Harlesden, Cardiff B&M). URL needs resolving.

articles.title: Cardiff man wrongly accused of shoplifting in B&M in facial recognition error
articles.notes: Cardiff, not London — comparative context. Retail FRS false positive. URL needs resolving.

articles.title: Met Police's use of facial recognition tech must comply with human rights law, says Equality and Human Rights Commission
articles.notes: EHRC calling on Met to ensure LFR compliance with human rights law. Regulator-level intervention — high accountability significance.

articles.title: Government expands police use of live facial recognition vans
articles.notes: August 2025 expansion — 10 mobile LFR vans rolled out across England & Wales. National context. URL needs resolving.

articles.title: Letters: The rapid expansion of biometric surveillance raises serious concerns about accuracy and ethics, but oversight is patchy and voluntary
articles.notes: Guardian letters page — civil society response to expansion. URL fragment incomplete — needs resolving.

articles.title: Facewatch and Manchester Met Police face lawsuits after facial recognition misidentification
articles.notes: Manchester, not London — but involves Facewatch, directly relevant to private-operators.json.

articles.title: Met Police gets first permanent facial recognition cameras in London
articles.notes: Confirms Croydon as first permanent static LFR installation — consistent with lfr-008. LBC article with 'sparking fears of...' framing.

articles.title: Police unlawfully storing images of innocent people for facial recognition
articles.notes: Unlawful image retention for FR watchlists. Legal dimension — relates to Bridges ruling requirement to delete non-matched faces immediately.

articles.title: London district calls on police to stop live facial recognition use — Islington (Arsenal FC stadium area)
articles.notes: Islington council/district calling for police to stop LFR around Emirates Stadium. Community resistance angle — maps directly onto community submissions layer. URL needs resolving to canonical Biometric Update article.

articles.title: FOIA request — Information about Facial Recognition (Met Police)
articles.notes: Jared's own FOIA request to Met Police. URL links to information about the request. Outcome/response not captured — follow up.

articles.title: Video doorbells, CCTV, facial recognition: how the police tracked UK rioters
articles.notes: August 2024 UK riots — retrospective FR use for identification. Different legal framework to live LFR, but relevant context for surveillance infrastructure overlap.
